title: Deere & Company (DE) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on Deere & Company using recent actuals. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what Deere is really worth. Economic Prospect Score: 84/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-xgg4qncd>
Deere operates in a cyclical industry. While precision ag tech provides secular tailwinds, high interest rates and normalizing farm incomes create headwinds. A 4% growth rate reflects modest expansion over a full cycle, balancing price increases against unit volume fluctuations.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use DCF instead of a P/E or EV/EBITDA multiple?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "Deere & Company (DE) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, March 18, 2026.

prospectVerdictDetail: Deere & Company benefits from an incredibly wide economic moat built on its dominant brand, extensive dealer network, and high switching costs. Its integration of precision agriculture technology further solidifies its competitive advantage, transforming it from a traditional equipment manufacturer into a tech-driven solutions provider. While cyclical headwinds exist, its long-term prospect remains strong.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Deere maintains its market leadership, driven by technological innovation and a strong global footprint.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs Peers
factors.rationale: While revenue growth is subject to agricultural cycles, Deere consistently outperforms peers through its premium product offerings and strong aftermarket sales.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: Deere holds a dominant market share in North American large agriculture and continues to expand its presence in key international markets.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: The company possesses significant pricing power, supported by its brand strength, technological superiority, and the critical nature of its equipment to farmers.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Innovation is steady, focusing on precision ag, autonomy, and electrification, although product cycles are longer compared to the tech sector.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Revenue declined ~12% in FY2025 as the agricultural equipment cycle turned down — lower crop prices reduced farmer equipment purchases. This is worse than AGCO (~-8%) and CNH Industrial (~-10%), reflecting Deere's larger exposure to the large ag market which is most cyclically sensitive. However, parts and service revenue remained resilient at +3%, providing a counter-cyclical buffer.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Deere's moat is exceptionally durable, fortified by its unmatched dealer network and the integration of proprietary technology.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Switching costs are extremely high. Farmers invest heavily in Deere's ecosystem, including equipment, software, and training, making switching to competitors highly disruptive and costly.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Its extensive and loyal dealer network creates a powerful network effect, ensuring unmatched service and support, which reinforces brand loyalty.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Deere holds a vast portfolio of patents, particularly in precision agriculture and autonomous farming technologies, creating significant barriers to entry.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: While capital intensive, Deere's scale and operational efficiency allow it to generate strong returns on invested capital over the business cycle.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Sentiment is currently mixed due to near-term cyclical concerns, but long-term catalysts remain intact.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Estimates face pressure during cyclical downturns in the agricultural sector, impacting short-term sentiment.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative is increasingly focused on Deere's transformation into an ag-tech leader, highlighting its advancements in autonomy and precision farming.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management has a proven track record of navigating cycles effectively and returning capital to shareholders through dividends, with a recent increase announced.

prospectKeyRisks: High sensitivity to global agricultural commodity prices and farm income levels.
Geopolitical tensions and trade disputes that could impact global demand for agricultural products.
Increasing competition in the precision agriculture and autonomous farming space.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Agricultural equipment cycle recovery in FY2027-2028 as crop prices stabilize, farmer balance sheets remain healthy, and deferred equipment purchases create pent-up demand for a replacement cycle
Precision agriculture technology adoption reaching inflection — autonomous tractors, See & Spray, and data-driven agronomic services generating $1B+ in annual software/subscription revenue by 2028
Construction equipment share gains in compact and mid-size segments through technology differentiation and dealer network expansion, diversifying revenue and reducing ag-cycle dependency
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored DE at 82/100 and Opus at 70/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
